Empires & Puzzles Elba Review: 5-Star Legendary Fire Red from Cupid Family

Elba is a walking paradox: she dares you to dispel her buffs. If you do, she heals her team for a massive amount. If you don't, her counterattack punishes you. Is this unique 'damned if you do, damned if you don't' mechanic enough to keep her relevant in today's high-stat meta? Read our full review.
Elba: The Love Trap You Can't Dispel
Elba, The Stalwart Protector of Love: Punishment, Patience, and Payback
Elba is a 5-star Fire (Red) hero from the

event, released in early 2024. As a Paladin, she is built to survive, which pairs perfectly with her kit's central theme: punishment. Elba isn't your typical healer or tank; she plays a psychological game with the opponent, forcing them to choose between taking damage from her counterattack or triggering a massive heal for her team by dispelling it. She's essentially a "Trojan Horse" of health—you break the shield, and the healing pours out.

Elba’s design is a striking blend of a warrior and a valentine. She sports ornate red and white armor adorned with rose patterns and carries a heart-shaped shield that looks like a box of chocolates you definitely shouldn't try to eat. It's a fun, thematic design that screams "Defender of Love," though that sword suggests her version of love involves a fair bit of sharp steel.

While respectable for her time, these defensive stats are now overshadowed by newer tanks boasting 1300+ Defense and 2200+ Health.
However, her Paladin class helps mitigate this stat gap slightly, giving her extra defense boosts when hit. But make no mistake: you are bringing her for the

She forces the enemy to hold their dispels. If they use a dispel (like Tyrum Glass or a modern dispeller) to get rid of the annoying counterattack, they instantly heal your team for 1300 HP—which is essentially a full heal for many heroes.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Cupid family and seasonal passives add significant utility that helps keep Elba viable despite her aging stats.
This bonus is fantastic for defense teams. If Elba is the only Cupid hero on your team (which is common), she gets
healing, making her even harder to take down.
This is huge. She ignores silence effects (like Peters or Miki) and Mindless Attack. She will
cast her skill when charged, ensuring her trap is set.
Gaining 5% mana when receiving an ailment makes her faster against DoT-heavy or debuff-heavy teams.
is the perfect Aether Power for a tank. Reducing all incoming damage by 10% for the first 6 turns gives her the durability she needs to survive long enough to fire her special at least once.

Upon release, Elba was a "thinker's hero." She wasn't an instant "I win" button like some OP heroes, but players who understood how to leverage her dispel punishment found great success in War Equalizer.
Her raw stats have fallen behind. A 325% hit is no longer threatening to modern monsters with 1300+ Defense. However,
. Her ability to punish dispellers remains unique and useful.
She is no longer a universal defense hero. However, she remains a top-tier pick for
and a strong counter-pick in raids against heavy dispeller teams.
Elba is a tank/support hybrid. You need her alive to keep the "threat" of the heal active.
Maximizes her survivability. The longer she lives, the more likely the enemy is to be forced into a bad decision (dispelling) or suffering through the counterattack.
Increases the damage of her hit and slightly increases counterattack damage (since she takes more damage due to lower defense).
She might die before the 4-turn timer pops, wasting the heal potential entirely.
. Elba's job isn't to kill you with her sword; it's to kill you with frustration and counterattacks. Keep her alive.
One of the few heroes in the game that actually benefits from being dispelled.
The War Equalizer effect counts as "removing" the buff, meaning she heals her team for 1300 HP automatically every time the Equalizer triggers (if timed correctly).
Passive resistance to Special Skill blocking means she almost always gets to do her job.
If the enemy doesn't dispel, 4 turns is a long time to wait for a heal. She can die before it triggers.
If an enemy casts "Block Buffs" (like Buff Block from Hipnos or others)
she fires, she can't place her buffs, rendering her special useless.
She is squishy compared to 2026 heroes and relies heavily on emblems and limit breaks to survive.
This is her playground. The automatic cleanse triggers her max heal constantly.
Average speed becomes Very Fast. Constant counterattack and healing traps are a nightmare to deal with.
Can still hold Diamond arena cups effectively against players who rely on dispellers.

bring a dispeller for Ludwig, but using it triggers Elba's massive heal.
In Equalizer war, fire Elba right before the Equalizer turn. The game removes her buff -> Boom, 1300 HP heal for everyone. It turns the war effect into a dedicated healer for your team.

While stealing the buff removes it from her (triggering the heal for
team?), stealing the counterattack is annoying.
Note on mechanics: Stealing usually counts as removing, so it should trigger the heal, but you give the enemy the counterattack.

If you pulled Elba, congratulations! You have a very sophisticated tank. Focus on keeping her alive. Don't worry about her damage; look at her as a healer who also hurts people who touch her. She is an excellent center for your defense team until you get modern meta tanks.
Elba is a "pocket pick." You probably won't use her in your main war defense anymore due to stats. However, for

, she is absolutely worth keeping on your roster. Do not feed her away. Is she worth an Alpha Aether? Only if you lack a dedicated tank for your Equalizer war team. Otherwise, save the resources for newer stats.
"The worst hero in the portal, imho."
- An early forum reaction that aged poorly once people realized the power of the dispel trigger.
"It’s a Tremor style effect removal. Which means if she fires again when the buff is effected, it procs the 1300 hp."
- A sharp-eyed player noticing the refresh mechanic.
"She works great with with Odette and even better with Oddille to bypass the 4 turns - pretty quick for a 1300hp boost."
- Players finding creative ways to manually trigger the heal.

Elba is the embodiment of "passive-aggressive" gameplay. She doesn't burst you down; she invites you to hit her, invites you to dispel her, and then laughs as her health bar fills back up. While her stats are gathering dust, her mechanic is timeless. In the right hands, specifically in Equalizer wars, she is still a heartbreaker.
A mechanic-heavy hero who survives the test of time better than pure damage dealers, but keep her away from raw stat battles.
A detailed review of Elba, the 5-star Fire hero from Empires & Puzzles Season of Love. We analyze her unique delayed healing mechanic, counterattack trap, and viability in the modern meta.
